reforms.description: The Dutch authorities introduced institutional and tax reforms to enhance the supply of risk capital and support the commercialization of private research while implementing measures to reduce impediments to entrepreneurship, including phasing out the Establishment Law for certain self-employed sectors and streamlining tax requirements to enhance technological start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance risk capital availability and reduce barriers to entrepreneurship, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice and are actively supporting innovation and entrepreneurship in the Netherlands.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at enhancing the innovation ecosystem and supporting entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities are taking measures to reduce impediments to entrepreneurship, such as high administrative burdens, complicated subsidy schemes, and growth barriers for potential high-growth companies.
